free_text: Measurement of the cross sections for the 238U(n,2n)
D-T reaction by bombarding a TiT target with
analyzed deuteron beam from  a Cockcroft-Walton
accelerator. Neutron energy was varied by changing
the  emission angle to the deuteron beam.
Activity of the residual nuclei for the
56Fe(n,p) reactions have been determined by a
Ge(Li) - detector of 40 cm3 having energy resolution
Monitoring of the time variation of the
neutron flux was performed either by the fission
chamber or a 6LiI(Eu3) or stilbene detector. These
data were then used to compute the corrections for
the initial activities of different half-lives.
Activity of the Al and Cu foils has also been
determined by 4Pi-beta counting using a flow-type
proportional counter. The efficiency was measured by
The following corrections were applied to the
measured fission  events: losses in pulses due to the
discriminator threshold  calculated from pulse height
fragments, and fragment self-absorption in the  layer.
